## Load a cross-sectional antibody survey data set {.unnumbered}


### Description

Load a cross-sectional antibody survey data set



### Usage

<pre><code class='language-R'>load_pop_data(file_path, ...)
</code></pre>


### Arguments

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="file_path">file_path</code></td>
<td>
path to an RDS file containing a cross-sectional antibody
survey data set, stored as a <code>data.frame()</code> or tibble::tbl_df
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="...">...</code></td>
<td>

Arguments passed on to <code>as_pop_data</code>


<dl>
<dt><code>data</code></dt><dd>a <code>data.frame()</code> or tibble::tbl_df
</dd>
<dt><code>antigen_isos</code></dt><dd><code>character()</code>
vector of antigen isotypes to be used in analyses
</dd>
<dt><code>age</code></dt><dd>a <code>character()</code> identifying the age column
</dd>
<dt><code>id</code></dt><dd>a <code>character()</code> identifying the id column
</dd>
<dt><code>value</code></dt><dd>a <code>character()</code> identifying the value column
</dd>
<dt><code>standardize</code></dt><dd>a <code>logical()</code> to determine standardization of columns
</dd>
</dl>
</td></tr>
</table>


### Value

a <code>pop_data</code> object (a tibble::tbl_df with extra attributes)



### Examples
```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, eval=TRUE}
library("serocalculator")

xs_data <- load_pop_data(serocalculator_example("example_pop_data.rds"))

print(xs_data)


```
